name: Bacon and Egg Tacos
instructions:
1. Place the bacon strips in a cold skillet and set over medium heat. Cook the bacon, flipping occasionally, until it becomes crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes total. The bacon will render its fat as it cooks. Transfer the cooked bacon to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess grease. Once cooled slightly, crumble the bacon into bite-sized pieces and set aside. In a medium bowl, crack the eggs and whisk them together until the yolks and whites are fully combined. Stir the crumbled bacon into the whisked eggs.

2. Melt the butter in a clean skillet over medium heat, swirling to coat the bottom evenly. Pour in the egg and bacon mixture. Let it sit undisturbed for about 30 seconds to begin setting, then gently scramble with a spatula, pushing the eggs from the edges toward the center. Cook until the eggs are just set but still slightly creamy, about 2 to 3 minutes total. Do not overcook or the eggs will become rubbery. Remove from heat and immediately stir in the American cheese, allowing it to melt into the hot eggs. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.

3. Wrap the tortillas in damp paper towels to keep them from drying out. Microwave on high for 30 seconds to 1 minute until they are warm and pliable. This makes them easier to fold without cracking.

4. Spoon about one quarter cup of the egg mixture into the center of each warmed tortilla. Fold the bottom edge up over the filling, then fold in the sides to create a taco shape. Serve immediately while hot, accompanied by your favorite salsa for topping or dipping.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook bacon until crispy, drain, crumble, and mix with whisked eggs.

2. Scramble the egg mixture in melted butter, then stir in cheese and season.

3. Wrap tortillas in damp paper towels and microwave until warm.

4. Fill each tortilla with egg mixture, fold, and serve with salsa.
